title: Wedelia rudis
taxonomy: Wedelia rudis is classified within the Asteraceae family, which comprises flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. This family is one of the largest and most widespread plant families globally. Within the Asteraceae family, Wedelia rudis falls under the subfamily Asteroideae, which is a significant group of flowering plants. Further classification places Wedelia rudis within the tribe Heliantheae, a group of plants that share similar characteristics. The complete classification is: Wedelia rudis (family: Asteraceae, subfamily: Asteroideae, tribe: Heliantheae).
uses: Wedelia rudis has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and flowers are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help in treating wounds and infections. The plant is also believed to have antiseptic and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In some cultures, Wedelia rudis is used to treat fever and cough, and its leaves are also used as a poultice to reduce swelling. However, further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Wedelia rudis for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Wedelia rudis has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues. It is known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help in reducing swelling and preventing infections. Its leaves are also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and promote overall well-being.

constituents: Wedelia rudis contains various active constituents, including triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds have been found to possess potential therapeutic benefits, such as reducing inflammation and combating oxidative stress. Additionally, Wedelia rudis contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have been reported to exhibit antimicrobial and cytotoxic activities. The plant's active constituents may also play a role in its traditional uses, such as treating skin conditions and respiratory issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the mechanisms of action of these compounds in Wedelia rudis.

preparations: Wedelia rudis is used in herbal preparations, primarily for its medicinal properties. intro: Wedelia rudis, also known as the rough wedelia or hairy wedelia, is a perennial plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties, providing relief from fever, colds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Wedelia rudis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Wedelia rudis is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the form of capsules or tablets to alleviate symptoms of fever and respiratory tract infections.
